events.title: US Disables Cargo Ship in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. forces disabled a small cargo ship on May 29 after it ignored warnings, according to CENTCOM. The incident occurred in the Gulf of Oman, a key maritime chokepoint.

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian port and oil depot
events.description: Ukrainian drone strikes damaged a port and oil refinery in Taganrog and an oil depot in Armavir, disrupting Russian oil infrastructure and potentially affecting regional freight and logistics flows.

events.title: Rio de Janeiro port upgrades for larger vessels
events.location: Port of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
events.description: The Port of Rio de Janeiro has completed a USD 32 million dredging and engineering project, allowing it to accommodate vessels up to 366 metres in length and increasing its operational capacity.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure impacts fleet operations
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz enters its fourth month, affecting 16 Anglo-Eastern vessels and over 350 seafarers. The company is enhancing fleet security via its Global Security Desk to monitor threats and support crews.

events.title: Porsche Wins Lawsuit Over Felicity Ace Fire
events.description: A German court ruled in favor of Porsche in a lawsuit regarding the fire on the car carrier Felicity Ace, which caused significant cargo loss. The cause of the fire remains unproven, impacting maritime freight and insurance claims.

events.title: U.S. Warns Ships in Hormuz of Hostile Treatment
events.description: The U.S. has warned commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz that ignoring military orders may result in being treated as hostile targets, raising concerns about operational safety and potential disruptions to shipping.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz standoff, now in its fourth month, involves mutual blockades with Iran charging ships up to $2 million for safe passage, while the U.S. enforces its own restrictions, causing significant disruption to global shipping.

events.title: EU plans new steel tariffs, negotiations with China
events.description: The European Union plans to implement new steel tariffs starting July 1, prompting negotiations with China under the World Trade Organization. China's Ministry of Commerce has criticized the move as trade protectionism.

events.title: Increased dark transits in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Vortexa has observed a significant rise in dark transits through the Strait of Hormuz, with 57% of all transits since March being dark. The trend reflects heightened uncertainty and potential risks to global oil and freight flows.

events.title: Russian attacks on merchant ships near Odesa injure crew
events.description: A Turkish-owned cargo ship was set on fire, and two crew members were injured near Odesa as Russia continues attacks on merchant vessels in the region.

events.title: US ports face $6.7bn investment gap
events.description: The National Association of Waterfront Employers (NAWE) reports a $6.7 billion investment gap in US ports, threatening competitiveness and supply chain resilience. The survey highlights urgent modernization and infrastructure needs across major ports.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Disruptions Drain Oil Inventories
events.description: The heads of four major global economic and energy institutions issued a joint warning about ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which are rapidly depleting oil inventories at a record pace.

events.title: COSCO Tanker Arrives in Connecticut under Jones Act Waiver
events.description: A Chinese-owned COSCO tanker delivered asphalt to Connecticut under a Trump-era Jones Act waiver, reigniting debate over the policy's impact on maritime trade and supply chain operations.

events.title: Restricted Area for Drone Performance in Hong Kong
events.location: New Hung Hom Harbourfront, Hong Kong
events.description: A restricted area will be in place off the New Hung Hom Harbourfront in Hong Kong from 1800 to 2200 hours on May 30, 2026, for a drone performance. This may cause minor disruptions to vessel movements in the area during the event.

events.title: Cable Layer Collides with Boxship off Pakistan
events.description: A cable layer was holed in a collision with a containership off the coast of Pakistan on May 28. Officials are investigating the incident, which occurred as both vessels were maneuvering outside the port area.

events.title: Evergreen and T.S. Lines slot swap on Asia-Japan services
events.description: Evergreen Marine Corporation and T.S. Lines have entered into a slot swap agreement on their JPI and JHTN services connecting China, Taiwan, and Japan. The arrangement allows both carriers to expand network coverage without deploying additional vessels.

events.title: Vessels attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Several vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz have been attacked in recent days, highlighting significant risks to global shipping and supply chain flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping status in question
events.description: President Trump claimed a breakthrough regarding the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting Iran must allow unrestricted commercial shipping. However, questions remain about the actual status and enforceability of shipping access through this critical global trade chokepoint.

events.title: Big Oil Tankers Escape Iran War Blockade
events.description: Roughly one-quarter of non-Iranian large oil tankers trapped in the Persian Gulf due to the Iran war have managed to escape in a slow, stealthy manner.

events.title: UK bans Russian LNG maritime transport
events.description: The UK introduced new sanctions prohibiting the maritime transportation of Russian liquefied natural gas (LNG), strengthening its existing restrictions on Russian energy exports.

events.title: Ocean Alliance revises CBX service route
events.description: The Ocean Alliance has revised the Chesapeake Bay Express (CBX) service, adding Port Kelang back to the rotation and including New York. The service is operated by CMA CGM on behalf of the alliance.

events.title: Medkon launches Far East–Mediterranean service
events.description: Medkon Lines is set to launch a new Far East–Mediterranean connection with its BTN service, starting with a single sailing from Shanghai and Ningbo to Mediterranean ports.

events.title: EU customs rule change risks parcel disruption
events.description: DHL, FedEx, and UPS warn that the EU's new customs rules for low-value parcels, including the removal of the €150 de minimis exemption and a €3 flat-rate tax, could cause border disruptions starting 1 July.

events.title: Freight rate spike expected due to global supply chain pressures
events.description: Freight rates are expected to rise significantly as global supply chain disruptions intensify, with carriers likely to benefit from increased demand and constrained liner networks.

events.title: Shipping carriers impose 'pay to play' pricing on contracts
events.description: Shipping carriers are enforcing peak season surcharges on long-term contracts due to high demand and tight capacity, forcing shippers to pay higher prices to secure freight space. Forwarders warn that spot rates on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes may rise sharply in the coming weeks.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire extension optimism
events.description: Growing optimism over a potential US-Iran deal to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is weighing on energy markets, with implications for global oil supply stability.

events.title: Houston Ship Channel vessel activity rises to seven-year high
events.description: Vessel activity along the Houston Ship Channel has increased by 5% compared to the same period in 2025, with over 2,000 arrivals recorded in Q1 2026, reflecting growing cargo volumes through the channel.

events.title: US warns Oman on Hormuz Strait toll proposal
events.description: The U.S. warned Oman against participating in a proposed toll system for the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The warning highlights potential policy shifts that could disrupt maritime trade flows through the region.

events.title: Tight bunker fuel availability in Houston
events.description: Bunker fuel availability in the US Gulf port of Houston has become tight, with suppliers requiring reconfirmation of offers due to ongoing market volatility. Demand remains strong, particularly affecting HSFO and LSMGO lead times.

events.title: ARA bunker availability tightens due to Hormuz disruption
events.description: Ongoing disruption near the Strait of Hormuz has caused loading delays and fuel blending issues, tightening bunker availability in the ARA region. Lead times for VLSFO and HSFO have doubled, with buyers advised to book 10 days in advance for competitive offers.

events.title: Increased Hormuz Strait ship transits on May 26
events.description: Ship transits through the Strait of Hormuz increased to 13 on May 26, with seven ships inbound and six outbound, according to CAS data.

events.title: Chinese aid ship arrives in Cuba
events.description: A bulk cargo ship carrying aid from China has arrived in Cuba, continuing supply efforts amid U.S. pressure on the Cuban government.

events.title: CMA CGM increases Asia-Mediterranean FAK rates
events.description: CMA CGM announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for cargo from Asian main ports to Mediterranean and North African destinations, effective from 15 to 31 June 2026.

events.title: UP-NS Merger Application Under STB Review
events.description: The Surface Transportation Board has accepted the revised merger application from Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern for consideration, but requires supplemental information before proceeding. The decision holds environmental and other reviews in abeyance.

events.title: Congestion at JNPA's largest container terminal
events.description: PSA International's BMCT terminal at Nhava Sheva Port is experiencing cargo handling delays and gate congestion due to a surge in transhipment volumes, despite improvements at other JNPA terminals.

events.title: Felicity Ace Fire Lawsuit Dismissed
events.description: A court dismissed a lawsuit against Porsche related to the fire aboard the car carrier Felicity Ace. The incident had previously caused a major disruption in the transport of vehicles.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS for Africa, Mediterranean, Maldives routes
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) for routes from China and Asia to West Africa, the Mediterranean, and the Maldives, effective 1 June 2026, impacting dry and reefer cargo under short-term contracts.

events.title: Yiulian Dockyard Reopens with Laden Vessel Repair Capability
events.description: Hong Kong's Yiulian Dockyard has reopened after modernization, offering a unique capability to repair laden vessels. Operated by China Merchants Shipbuilding Industry, it is now positioned as a highly flexible repair facility in Asia.

events.title: Hormuz shipping crisis nears 100 days
events.description: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz shows no sign of resolution, with tensions between the US and Iran escalating. The ongoing situation continues to pose a significant risk to global shipping.

events.title: Fire at Ro/Pax Ferry in Port of Naples
events.description: A fire broke out aboard a moored ro/pax ferry at the Port of Naples, Italy. Firefighters have controlled the blaze, but the incident may impact port operations and ferry services.

events.title: Product Tanker Fire Off Bangladesh
events.description: A product tanker caught fire while anchored off Chittagong, Bangladesh. The Bangladesh Coast Guard is working to control the fire.

events.title: Transpacific container rates surge 80% post-Middle East conflict
events.description: Early Xeneta data shows average spot rates on the Transpacific trade into the US West Coast have risen over 80% compared to pre-Middle East conflict levels. This significant increase reflects ongoing global supply chain disruptions and heightened shipping costs.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure traps ships, reduces tanker traffic
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed for three months, trapping hundreds of ships and thousands of seafarers in the Persian Gulf and significantly reducing global seaborne tanker volumes.

events.title: Indian Crew Released After 10-Month Detention in Iran
events.description: India has secured the release of 10 Indian nationals who were detained in Iran for 10 months. The incident highlights ongoing tensions affecting maritime personnel and supply chain operations in the region.

events.title: Drone Attacks on Shadow Fleet Tankers in Black Sea
events.description: Three sanctioned shadow fleet tankers in the Black Sea were attacked by drones on May 28, disrupting maritime operations and raising concerns over regional security and supply chain stability.

events.title: Sanctioned Russian Tanker Diverts from Cuba Route
events.description: A sanctioned Russian-flag tanker carrying diesel appears to have diverted south from its original route to Cuba, likely in response to sanctions and to avoid enforcement actions. The rerouting reflects ongoing disruptions in global energy logistics due to geopolitical pressures.

events.title: Iran Demands $12B for Strait of Hormuz Ceasefire
events.description: Iran is reportedly demanding $12 billion up front from the Trump administration for a 60-day ceasefire extension, which could impact the security and flow of shipping through the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: USPS and letter carriers head to contract mediation
events.description: The U.S. Postal Service and its largest union of letter carriers are entering mediation to negotiate a new contract. The ongoing negotiations could impact postal service operations and delivery timelines.

events.title: U.S. and Iran Reach Ceasefire Outline Agreement
events.description: The United States and Iran have reached an outline agreement to extend their ceasefire, pending final approval. This development could impact regional supply chain stability and freight flows in the Middle East.

events.title: Mangalore SPM Operations Suspended
events.description: Operations at the Mangalore SPM are suspended due to the southwest monsoon, affecting port activities.

events.title: U.S.-Iran strikes near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged airstrikes near the Strait of Hormuz, raising tensions and reducing hopes for a near-term peace deal. The strikes occurred hours after U.S. President Trump downplayed prospects for an agreement to reopen the critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: U.S. Warns Oman Over Hormuz Strait Toll System
events.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned Oman that the U.S. is prepared to sanction any entity involved in facilitating an Iranian-linked tolling system in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over potential disruptions to a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: UAE steps up as administrator amid Hormuz crisis
events.description: The Hormuz crisis is disrupting established trade corridors, prompting the UAE to position itself as an administrator of last resort, leveraging regional instability to increase its strategic role in global trade.

events.title: Trucking firms face bankruptcy due to freight market pressures
events.description: A new wave of trucking firms is filing for bankruptcy due to ongoing challenges such as uneven freight demand, high operating costs, and narrow profit margins. This signals broader instability in the freight and logistics sector.

events.title: Maersk denies vessel transit through dangerous Mideast route
events.description: Maersk has denied a report that one of its vessels recently transited a volatile Mideast shipping route, raising concerns about potential disruptions in a key global chokepoint.

events.title: Increased container capacity on India-China trade routes
events.description: Container capacity between India and China is rising due to growing demand. Ningbo Ocean Shipping (NOS) has entered the market via a slot deal with X-Press Feeders, with recent vessel calls at Nhava Sheva Port.

events.title: Premier Alliance drops Algeciras calls in network restructure
events.description: The Premier Alliance is removing two weekly calls at Algeciras on its Asia-North Europe services as part of a network restructuring. The changes aim to improve transit times to Le Havre, Antwerp, and Felixstowe.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure drives shipping cost spike
events.description: Evergreen Marine warns customers of rising fuel costs due to the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with impacts expected to persist into Q4.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic nearly halted due to military escalation
events.description: Commercial shipping has largely avoided the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed US military strikes, causing significant disruption to global freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: US-Iran tensions threaten Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Renewed hostilities between the US and Iran have raised concerns about the stability of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The conflict weakens hopes for a near-term peace agreement, potentially disrupting oil shipments and global supply chains.

events.title: Somali piracy linked to Red Sea disruptions
events.description: The resurgence of Somali piracy is being connected to the Houthi standoff in the Red Sea, indicating a broader, multi-chokepoint disruption affecting global shipping routes.

events.title: ONE updates FE1 and FE3 services via Cape of Good Hope
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) has updated its FE1 and FE3 services on the Asia-North Europe trade route via the Cape of Good Hope to improve schedule reliability, expand coverage, and reduce transit times.

events.title: Israeli ministries oppose Zim-Hapag merger
events.description: Israeli Ministry of Economy and Ministry of Agriculture have joined opposition to the proposed $4.2 billion acquisition of ZIM Integrated Shipping Services by Hapag-Lloyd and FIMI fund, citing concerns over national security, supply chain resilience, and food security.

events.title: Intermodal congestion risks at US rail ramps
events.description: Rising intermodal traffic in the US is causing concerns about rail ramp congestion, potentially leading to detention and demurrage charges. Intermodal volumes in North America saw a slight decline in Q1, but growing pressures remain.

events.title: Container industry executive steps down amid US cartel probe
events.description: Singamas Container Holdings chairman Teo Siong Seng has stepped down from his role at PIL following a US Justice Department indictment for alleged collusion with Chinese container manufacturers in a cartel-like scheme.

events.title: Drone attacks hit three tankers off Turkey
events.description: Three tankers were hit by drones in international waters north of Turkey's Turkeli coast, disrupting shipping near the Bosphorus. All vessels were in ballast condition at the time of the attacks.

events.title: Hormuz Strait conflict escalates
events.description: The Hormuz shipping crisis is worsening as US forces strike Iranian targets, Iran launches drones and missiles, and commercial vessels are forced to turn back from transiting the strait.

events.title: ADNOC LNG shipment through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker from ADNOC has passed through the Strait of Hormuz, indicating increased energy flows through this critical shipping route.

events.title: Arctic LNG 2 Cargo Transits Northern Sea Route in May
events.description: An icebreaking LNG carrier transited eastbound along Russia’s Northern Sea Route in May, an unusual early-season voyage following a load from the Arctic LNG 2 project. The move reflects favorable ice conditions and efforts to maintain Asian LNG exports.

events.title: Expedition Cruise Ship Hondius Delayed in Rotterdam
events.description: The expedition cruise ship Hondius remains delayed in Rotterdam for additional cleaning after arriving with a skeleton crew. The delay is causing a temporary disruption in its scheduled operations.

events.title: Jones Act waiver extension sparks industry pushback
events.description: The American Maritime Partnership is urging President Trump to end the 150-day Jones Act waiver, claiming it is harming domestic shipping jobs and investment with minimal benefit to fuel prices.

events.title: PIL Chairman steps aside amid US container cartel case
events.description: SS Teo, chairman of Pacific International Lines (PIL), is stepping aside amid a US Justice Department investigation into a container shipping cartel. The case could have significant implications for the global shipping industry.

events.title: Samsung challenges Wan Hai over container fees
events.description: Samsung Electronics America has filed a complaint with the US Federal Maritime Commission against Wan Hai Lines over more than $1.2 million in pandemic-era detention and demurrage fees.

events.title: U.S. Sanctions Iran's Hormuz Strait Entity
events.description: The U.S. Treasury sanctioned Iran’s Persian Gulf Strait Authority, alleging it collaborates with the IRGC to extort commercial vessels, escalating tensions in the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Resurgence of piracy in western Indian Ocean and Gulf of Aden
events.location: Western Indian Ocean and Gulf of Aden
events.description: The Chair of the Djibouti Code of Conduct/ Jeddah Amendment reports a resurgence of piracy in the western Indian Ocean and Gulf of Aden region. While Somali piracy remains suppressed, the situation remains a concern for maritime security and supply chain operations.

events.title: Iraqi supertanker passes Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An Iraqi supertanker successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz, signaling potential easing of oil supply constraints that have impacted global markets and driven up US gasoline prices.

events.title: Iranian activity disrupts Hormuz Strait traffic
events.description: Three months into Operation Epic Fury, Hormuz transits remain at about 6% of pre-conflict levels, with increased Iranian naval presence and Iranian-flagged or dark vessels dominating the remaining traffic.

events.title: Strait remains closed with no deal
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed with no progress on reopening, continuing to disrupt global shipping and energy flows.

events.title: US-Iran conflict disrupts LNG supply to Asia
events.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict has caused a supply shortfall in Middle Eastern LNG exports, prompting Asian buyers to source more flexible Atlantic basin cargoes to compensate.

events.title: Hormuz LNG supply backlog to persist through 2027
events.description: LNG exporters in the Middle East may take years to resolve contractual disputes and missed deliveries after traffic through the Strait of Hormuz resumes, with a backlog potentially affecting cargo programming into 2027.

events.title: Iranian missiles attack Korean vessel in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The Korean government confirmed that Iranian anti-ship missiles attacked a Korean vessel in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month, based on an inspection by a government team. The incident raises concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade.

events.title: Ichthys LNG labor strike averted
events.description: The labor union for Australia's offshore oil and gas workers has called off plans for a strike at the Ichthys LNG terminal in Western Australia, avoiding a potential supply chain disruption.

events.title: Iranian Missile Components Found on HMM Ship
events.location: Namu cargo ship (Somewhere in Indian Ocean)
events.description: South Korean officials identified Iranian missile components on the HMM cargo ship Namu, following an attack. The incident raises concerns about maritime security and potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: U.S. strikes Iranian drone launcher near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. forces conducted a limited strike on an Iranian drone launcher in response to an attack on merchant shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz crisis forces shipping route redesign
events.description: CMA CGM has redesigned parts of its logistics network due to the ongoing Hormuz crisis, implementing alternative multimodal corridors to maintain cargo flows into Gulf countries.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd applies PSS on Far East to Europe routes
events.location: Far East to North Europe and Mediterranean
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd is introducing a peak season surcharge (PSS) on all container types for cargo moving from the Far East to North Europe and the Mediterranean, effective June 8, 2026, until further notice.

events.title: FMC penalizes Maersk for improper detention billing
events.description: The US Federal Maritime Commission has imposed a $1.9 million civil penalty on Maersk for allegedly billing detention charges to unauthorized third parties, violating US shipping regulations.

events.title: Agricultural voyage durations hit record highs
events.description: Climate disruptions, longer voyage distances, and pressure on key shipping routes are causing record voyage durations for agricultural shipments, reshaping dry bulk markets and increasing tonne-mile demand.

events.title: Bulk carriers converted to container ships
events.description: Shipowners are converting two supramax bulk carriers into 2,500 TEU container ships due to the tight boxship market, according to Alphaliner. This reflects significant industry activity in response to ongoing supply chain demand.

events.title: EU Labour Protections Gap for Seafarers
events.description: A new study reveals significant labour rights gaps for maritime crews in Europe, highlighting excessive working hours, fatigue risks, and weaker legal protections compared to land-based workers.

events.title: Legal battle over Hormuz benchmark impacts shipping markets
events.description: A fast-tracked legal trial between Mercuria Energy Group and the Baltic Exchange over the TD3C VLCC benchmark is set for October 26 in London, with potential major implications for tanker freight markets and shipping derivatives.

events.title: Iranian Missile Attack on Ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A cargo ship operated by South Korean shipper HMM was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, with South Korea stating the attack likely involved an Iranian anti-ship missile. The incident raises concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to global shipping.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Threatens Energy Supply
events.description: A U.S.-Israeli war on Iran could lead to prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz, potentially forcing global energy demand reductions, according to Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan.

events.title: GMS approved to scrap sanctioned ships
events.description: Dubai-based GMS has received U.S. government approval to scrap four container ships previously under Iran-related sanctions. This move could help reduce the shadow fleet and ease regulatory tensions.

events.title: Jones Act waiver allows foreign tankers in U.S. oil routes
events.description: The Trump administration's emergency Jones Act waiver is allowing foreign-flagged tankers to operate on domestic U.S. oil routes, significantly altering trade patterns and energy logistics.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Deal Remains Unresolved
events.description: President Donald Trump dismissed a report that Iran and Oman would manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as part of a deal to end the war, indicating ongoing uncertainty and lack of resolution for this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Russian drone targets sail training ship in Odesa
events.description: A Russian drone reportedly targeted a sail training ship in Odesa, raising concerns over the impact of military actions on maritime safety and regional supply chain stability.

events.title: MSC settles 2024 runaway ship incident in Charleston
events.description: MSC has settled out of court regarding a 2024 incident involving a runaway ship in Charleston. The event had previously raised concerns about port safety and operations.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Impacts Chinese Refiners
events.description: The ongoing Strait of Hormuz crisis has led to consistently low crude oil prices, with Chinese refiners absorbing much of the economic impact. The disruption is affecting global freight flows and energy markets.

events.title: CMA CGM imposes PCS due to Abidjan port congestion
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced a Port Congestion Surcharge (PCS) for cargo to Abidjan, Côte d’Ivoire, effective from 8 June 2026 for most regions, due to ongoing port congestion.

events.title: FMC fines Maersk $1.9M for detention billing
events.description: The U.S. Federal Maritime Commission has imposed a $1.9 million civil penalty on A.P. Moller - Maersk for allegedly improper detention charge billing practices, signaling regulatory action in the shipping industry.

events.title: Singapore Bunker Fuel Availability Tightens
events.description: Bunker fuel availability in Singapore remains tight, with limited HSFO supplies and extended lead times for VLSFO. Small parcel availability is constrained, and delivery times have increased.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Post-Ceasefire
events.description: AIS data from March 1 to April 21, 2026, shows how shipping traffic has evolved through the Strait of Hormuz following a recent conflict and ceasefire. The data reveals patterns of vessel usage and potential disruptions in this critical maritime chokepoint.

events.title: Seafarers strike against Eimskip ships
events.description: Seafarers represented by the Icelandic Seamen’s Union began a strike against three ships owned by Iceland’s Eimskip, impacting operations.

events.title: Oman Mediates Hormuz Strait Tensions
events.description: Oman is facilitating talks with Iran to resolve tensions affecting the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, amid ongoing diplomatic efforts.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Impacts Production
events.description: Delays in the Strait of Hormuz are causing downstream production losses as the chokepoint disruption affects global freight flows and energy markets.

events.title: Hormuz Strait disruption raises container rates
events.description: Intra-Asia container rates rose 2% to $959 per 40ft container due to ongoing disruption around the Strait of Hormuz. The Drewry Intra-Asia Container Index is now 75% higher than pre-conflict levels, driven by rerouting, higher bunker costs, and reduced effective capacity.

events.title: Chile approves major San Antonio port expansion
events.description: Chile has approved a $4.45 billion expansion of the San Antonio port, known as 'Puerto Exterior,' marking a significant infrastructure development expected to enhance regional freight capacity and efficiency.

events.title: Kandla Port Jetty Shutdown for Maintenance
events.description: IFFCO Jetty No.5 at Kandla Port, India, is closed for maintenance from May 26, 2026, until June 5, 2026, potentially affecting cargo operations.

events.title: Tanker Explosion Off Oman Coast
events.description: A tanker reported an external explosion on its port side near the waterline approximately 60 nautical miles from Muscat, Oman. Some bunker fuel was released into the sea, but the vessel and crew remained safe.

events.title: Greek Crude Oil Tanker Explosion Off Oman
events.description: A Greek crude oil supertanker reported an explosion and fuel leak off the coast of Oman near Muscat. The incident occurred amid heightened regional tensions following Iran's IRGC vow of revenge.

events.title: AGs urge rejection of UP-Norfolk Southern rail merger
events.description: A group of Attorneys General is urging the Surface Transportation Board to reject the proposed merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern, which could significantly impact rail freight operations across the U.S.

events.title: EU-Mexico Free Trade Agreement Signed
events.description: The EU and Mexico signed a new free-trade agreement, providing duty-free access for most goods and aiming to reduce reliance on the US. The deal expands on a 2000 agreement and is expected to boost trade flows between the regions.

events.title: UK Deploys Mine-Hunting Ship Near Hormuz
events.description: The UK has deployed the Royal Fleet Auxiliary vessel RFA Lyme Bay, a mine-hunting mothership, for a potential multinational mission in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: U.S. Denies Resuming Naval Escorts in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. Central Command denied reports that the U.S. Navy has restarted escorting commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz under the 'Project Freedom' operation, which had previously been suspended.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Vessel Transit Guidance
events.description: Hundreds of vessels remain unable to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Industry groups have issued guidance to mitigate navigational risks if normal conditions resume.

events.title: Crew strike begins on Eimskip vessels
events.description: An indefinite crew strike has begun on several vessels owned by Icelandic shipping line Eimskip, following failed wage negotiations. The strike, organized by the Icelandic Seamen’s Union, involves bosuns and able seamen and is impacting shipping operations.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Administered Access Normalizing
events.description: The Hormuz crisis is leading to administered access as reinsurers and P&I clubs restrict coverage, while Chinese-linked tankers receive selective authorization through the physically open strait.

events.title: Low water levels disrupt Rhine barge traffic
events.description: Falling water levels on the Rhine River, currently at 110cm with forecasts predicting a drop to 100cm, are threatening already strained barge services. Low water levels are causing delays and may lead to additional surcharges, similar to those imposed in 2023.

events.title: US and Iran Clash Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: US and Iranian forces clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in a critical global shipping chokepoint despite ongoing peace talks.

events.title: Mines Attached to LPG Tanker in Baltic Sea
events.description: Magnetic explosive devices were discovered attached to a Liberia-flagged liquefied petroleum gas tanker at the Russian Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga, in an incident described as a thwarted terrorist attack.

events.title: Tanker Explosion Off Oman Coast
events.description: A tanker reported an external explosion near its port side east of Oman, prompting the UK Maritime and Coastguard Agency (UKMTO) to issue a caution for ships in the area.

events.title: Middle East Crisis Disrupts Global Trade Flows
events.description: CMA CGM warns that the ongoing Middle East crisis continues to reshape global trade, causing disruptions in freight markets and shifting trade flows. The carrier reported navigating these challenges in Q1 2026.

events.title: US strikes Iran missile sites, raises regional tensions
events.description: US forces launched strikes on Iranian missile sites and suspected mine-laying boats in southern Iran, increasing uncertainty around ongoing ceasefire talks and potentially impacting regional freight flows.

events.title: Magnetic mines found on LPG tanker in Ust-Luga
events.description: Magnetic explosive devices were discovered attached to the hull of the Liberia-flagged LPG tanker Arrhenius at Russia's Ust-Luga port. The FSB reported the incident as a foiled terrorist attack.

events.title: OFAC approves ship scrapping for sanctioned vessels
events.description: The U.S. has approved Dubai-based GMS to purchase four sanctioned containerships for scrapping, potentially creating a legal exit for vessels in the shadow fleet and impacting global shipping compliance and fleet dynamics.

events.title: El Niño expected to worsen shipping disruptions
events.description: A strengthening El Niño weather pattern is expected to amplify existing shipping disruptions, compounding issues from the Hormuz crisis and Panama Canal congestion.

events.title: Iran deal hopes ease Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: European natural gas prices dropped as optimism grew over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal, which could ease tensions and reopen the critical Strait of Hormuz, a major global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Cargo Ship Fire Injures Eight in Hamburg
events.description: A cargo ship fire occurred in Hamburg, Germany, on May 24, injuring eight people. The incident involved a vessel docked at the port and required emergency response.

events.title: Clash reported in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A fatal skirmish was reported in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, following social media reports of explosions along Iran's coastline.

events.title: Middle East oil and LNG vessels exit Hormuz
events.description: Two LNG tankers and a supertanker carrying Iraqi crude have exited the Strait of Hormuz after being delayed due to the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has severely disrupted shipping through the region.

events.title: Iran expands PGSA claimed zone near Hormuz
events.description: Iran has expanded its claimed Protected Green Shipping Area (PGSA) zone to the UAE coast, heightening tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. This development occurs amid ongoing diplomatic and military activity in the region, affecting global shipping flows.

events.title: US-Iran draft deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A draft preliminary agreement between the US and Iran outlines plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, ease sanctions, and extend a ceasefire. The deal could significantly impact global shipping through this critical oil chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Deal Framework Lacks Specifics
events.description: Iran and the U.S. have outlined a potential deal to resolve tensions, but a proposed memorandum does not include specifics on managing the Strait of Hormuz, leaving uncertainty over this critical chokepoint for global shipping.

events.title: Maharashtra Minor Ports Closure for Foul Weather Season
events.description: Minor ports in Maharashtra state, India (except Jaigad), will be closed for shipping activities and vessel navigation from May 26 to August 31, 2026, due to the foul weather season.

events.title: Mines found on LPG carrier arriving at Ust-Luga
events.description: Russian authorities discovered two mines attached to the hull of an LPG carrier arriving at the Ust-Luga port, indicating a potential security threat to maritime operations.

events.title: UAE develops Hormuz bypass strategy
events.description: The UAE is actively developing a maritime bypass strategy around the Strait of Hormuz, driven by validated bypass corridors, Iran's targeting logic, and a rebalancing of UAE port operations. This shift is reshaping Gulf maritime logistics in response to geopolitical and operational pressures.

events.title: US LNG vessels heading to China after long hiatus
events.description: Four Qatari LNG vessels loaded in the US are signaling the Tianjin terminal in China as their destination, marking a potential resumption of US LNG exports to China after more than a year of no trade. However, there is uncertainty about whether they will ultimately deliver to other markets.

events.title: Cape of Good Hope route surge impacts LPG shipping
events.description: The Cape of Good Hope route captured ~43% of US-to-Asia LPG voyages in April 2026, driven by high Panama Canal prices and delays, tightening VLGC supply and increasing freight rates on the BLPG3 route.

events.title: Panama Canal prepares for El Niño water shortages
events.description: The Panama Canal is preparing for potential water shortages due to an expected El Niño weather pattern, which could impact canal operations and global shipping flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic restrictions disrupt tanker deliveries
events.description: Traffic restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz, ongoing for over two months due to the Middle East war, are shifting tanker market priorities toward faster delivery times. Owners are paying higher resale premiums for ships that can begin operations sooner.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic resumes
events.description: Satellite tracking data showed three supertankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz, the first such crossings since the conflict began, signaling a potential easing of tensions in the critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: PSA Mumbai enhances rail freight connectivity with CONCOR
events.description: PSA India, in partnership with CONCOR, is improving rail freight connectivity through the Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) to enhance cargo movement efficiency and supply chain connectivity in North India.

events.title: Maersk skips Sydney on Olga Maersk OC1 voyage
events.description: Maersk has announced that the Olga Maersk will omit Sydney on voyages 618S southbound and 623N northbound on the OC1 service due to an operational delay en route to Oceania ports, necessitating schedule recovery.

events.title: SS Teo steps back amid container cartel allegations
events.description: Teo Siong Seng, executive chairman of Singamas Container Holdings, has stepped back from public roles as he faces U.S. Department of Justice allegations related to a global container cartel. The case could have significant implications for the container shipping industry.

events.title: Magnetic Mines Found on Tanker at Ust-Luga Port
events.description: Several NATO-produced magnetic mines were detected on a tanker at Russia's Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga, according to the Russian Investigative Committee.

events.title: Fishing vessel collides with container ship in North Sea
events.location: Dutch sector of the North Sea
events.description: On May 21, a small fishing vessel collided with a 230,000 dwt container ship in the Dutch sector of the North Sea. The smaller vessel sustained significant damage, raising concerns about safety and potential disruptions in the region.

events.title: Kenya Acquires New Ferry for Likoni Crossing
events.description: Kenya has acquired a new ferry to address safety and capacity issues at the Likoni ferry crossing in Mombasa, a key transport link that has long faced operational challenges.

events.title: Sinotrans orders 12 containerships
events.description: Sinotrans, part of China Merchants Energy Shipping, has ordered 12 containerships across three affiliated shipyards. Deliveries are set to begin in 2027, with vessel capacities ranging from 8,200 to 16,000 TEU.

events.title: Cosco revives Singapore-Calcutta SKX1 service
events.description: COSCO SHIPPING Lines has reinstated its SKX1 direct shuttle service between Singapore and Kolkata to enhance regional connectivity. The service aims to strengthen freight flows in the region following its earlier suspension.

events.title: South Korea's first NSR trial voyage
events.description: Panstar Line has been selected to conduct South Korea's first government-backed trial voyage along the Arctic Northern Sea Route (NSR), marking a potential shift in shipping routes and logistics strategy.

events.title: High AIS Disruption Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Unprecedented AIS disruption levels have been reported near the Strait of Hormuz in March 2026, indicating significant constraints on vessel mobility in the Gulf region. The disruptions are likely linked to geopolitical tensions and are impacting container shipping operations.

events.title: Baltic corridor under pressure from attacks
events.description: Russia’s Baltic export infrastructure, a key western export route, is under sustained attack, creating a new pressure point in global supply chains.

events.title: Iran tightens control over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran's increasing control over the Strait of Hormuz is causing disruptions to global shipping and energy markets. A full reopening of the waterway by 2026 is deemed unlikely, potentially forcing major importers to negotiate bilateral deals with Iran.

events.title: Asia-US container rates rise due to Iran war stalemate
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US have increased by about $1,000/FEU since the start of the Iran war due to ongoing tensions affecting key shipping routes. Tanker rates have softened across most trade lanes.

events.title: Potential Hormuz Strait reopening timeline
events.description: A potential end to the Middle East conflict raises questions about the timeline for normal tanker traffic to resume through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil transit chokepoint.

events.title: Ukrainian drone strike on Russian frigate Admiral Essen
events.description: Ukrainian drone forces claim to have attacked a Russian frigate and a hovercraft-like missile craft at a pier in Novorossiysk. The strike could impact regional military operations and potentially disrupt nearby maritime logistics.

events.title: Iraqi Crude Supertanker Exits Persian Gulf
events.description: A supertanker carrying Iraqi crude to China has exited the Persian Gulf and crossed into the Arabian Sea amid ongoing US-imposed blockade discussions.

events.title: Drone strike causes fire at Russian oil terminal
events.description: A drone strike ignited a fire at a major oil export terminal in Novorossiysk, Russia, during an overnight attack. The incident caused at least one injury and disrupted operations at the key Black Sea port.

events.title: Argentina reduces wheat, barley export duties
events.description: Argentina will reduce export duties for wheat and barley to 5.5% from 7.5% starting in June, aiming to ease pressure on producers ahead of the 2026-27 season. Soybean duties will also decrease from 7.5% to 5.5% in January 2027.

events.title: US-Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US and Iran are nearing a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz for commercial shipping and allow Iran to resume unrestricted oil exports as part of a 60-day ceasefire extension.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening Deal Near
events.description: The U.S. and Iran have outlined an agreement to extend their ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The deal has not yet been finalized.

events.title: Cocaine seizure on US-bound tanker disrupts freight
events.description: US authorities seized 227 kg of cocaine valued at $6.4m from a crude oil tanker arriving in Southern California. A Filipino seafarer has been charged in connection with the alleged cartel-linked smuggling operation.

events.title: Asia-Europe freight rates surge, WCI rises 6%
events.description: The Drewry World Container Index increased 6% to $2,712 per 40ft container, driven by a 15% rise in Asia-Europe spot rates, particularly from Shanghai to Rotterdam, signaling early peak season demand.

events.title: Chinese Coast Guard Ship Departs Pratas Islands Waters
events.description: A Chinese coast guard ship left waters near Taiwan's Pratas Islands after a tense standoff, raising concerns over regional tensions that could impact maritime freight routes.

events.title: LNG Tanker Exits Hormuz for India
events.description: An LNG tanker has exited the Strait of Hormuz heading to India for the first time since the war began, signaling a potential easing or shift in regional shipping disruptions.

events.title: Oman's strategic role in Hormuz dynamic
events.description: Oman is playing a pivotal role in the evolving Hormuz dynamic, influencing the potential permanence of the PGSA toll regime and affecting global maritime freight flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Russian Sheskharis oil terminal
events.description: The Ukrainian military reported striking Russia's Sheskharis oil terminal and nearby Grushova oil depot on the Black Sea, potentially disrupting oil infrastructure and regional supply chain operations.
